food_name: Vermentino di Gallura DOCG Superiore White Wine

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general white wine class (FOODON_03301636); no specific entry for Vermentino di Gallura DOCG exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code GE0LM represents white wine generically; no Vermentino di Gallura-specific code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 35748005 covers wine as a substance; no DOCG-specific or Vermentino-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 148 g (approximately 150 ml) glass of Vermentino di Gallura DOCG Superiore white wine, as typically poured in a restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: White wine, table — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174837)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for generic dry white table wine used as the closest approved analogue for Vermentino di Gallura DOCG Superiore. Vermentino di Gallura is a dry white wine with alcohol content approximately 13–14% ABV and residual sugar below 4 g/L, consistent with the USDA white table wine profile. Values are per 100 g of wine. No Singapore HPB, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry exists for this Italian DOCG wine.

gi_source.title: Alcoholic beverages — white wine glycemic index estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vermentino di Gallura DOCG Superiore. White wine as a category has a GI of approximately 45 (low) per the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables published in Diabetes Care. Vermentino di Gallura Superiore is a dry white wine with low residual sugar (< 4 g/L), consistent with the low-GI white wine category. Glycemic load per 148 g serving = (45 × 2.6 × 1.48) / 100 = 1.73, rounded to 1.8.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit alcohol intake to no more than one standard drink per day, as regular alcohol consumption raises blood pressure; a 150 ml glass of Vermentino di Gallura at 13–14% ABV contains approximately 15–17 g of ethanol, which approaches or exceeds recommended daily limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and low residual sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although Vermentino di Gallura DOCG Superiore is a dry white wine with low residual sugar and a low glycemic index of approximately 45, people with Type 2 Diabetes must exercise caution because ethanol inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis and can cause delayed hypoglycaemia, particularly when consumed without food or in combination with insulin or sulphonylurea medications.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol content, polyphenol content, and caloric contribution
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While moderate white wine consumption has been associated with modest cardiovascular benefits in observational studies, current clinical guidelines do not recommend initiating alcohol consumption for cardiovascular protection; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should consult their cardiologist before consuming alcohol, and the approximately 82 kcal per 100 g caloric density of this wine should be factored into overall dietary management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vermentino di Gallura DOCG Superiore is a premium Italian dry white wine produced in the Gallura zone of northern Sardinia, Italy, from the Vermentino grape variety. The DOCG (Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita) designation is the highest Italian wine classification, and the Superiore designation requires a minimum alcohol content of 14% ABV. The Monteoro label is produced by Sella & Mosca, one of Sardinia's most prominent and historically significant wineries. The wine presents a straw-yellow colour with greenish hues, an intense aromatic profile featuring notes of exotic fruit, white flowers, and Mediterranean scrub (macchia mediterranea), and a soft, long, and persistent palate with vegetal hints and mineral notes characteristic of the granite-rich Gallura terroir. Nutritionally, it is a low-residual-sugar dry wine with approximately 82 kcal per 100 g, negligible protein (0.07 g), no fat, and approximately 2.6 g of carbohydrate per 100 g, of which less than 1 g is sugar. The primary caloric contribution comes from ethanol (approximately 10–11 g per 100 g at 13–14% ABV). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low) based on the white wine category in the University of Sydney GI Database. Clinically, this wine is relevant for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its alcohol content; moderate consumption may be permissible for some patients but should always be discussed with a healthcare provider. It contains sulphites as a declared allergen. It is not considered halal or vegan (fining agents may include animal-derived products). In Singapore, this wine is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk and is priced at approximately SGD 97.19 per bottle.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy generic dry white table wine entry (FDC ID 174837), which is the closest approved analogue available. No direct laboratory analysis of Vermentino di Gallura DOCG Superiore Monteoro by Sella & Mosca is available in any approved primary source. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database white wine category. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy white wine entry; wine contains no fat by nature (naturally zero). 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero as wine contains no lipid fraction. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as wine is a fermented liquid with no dietary fibre. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero; wine contains no cholesterol. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy white wine entry. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy white wine entry. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy white wine entry. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy white wine entry. 9. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy white wine entry. 10. Input food_group was incorrectly listed as 'Grains and staples'; corrected to 'Beverages' as this is an alcoholic beverage. 11. Cuisine tags 'Pasta', 'Pizza', 'Chicken Dish' in the input reflect the restaurant's broader menu and are not attributes of this wine product. 12. The 2018 vintage year is noted in the original food name; the canonical food name has been normalised to remove the vintage year as nutrient values are vintage-independent. 13. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian DOCG wine; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese language requirements apply under G6 for an Italian cuisine item. 14. glycemic_index set to 45 (low) based on analogous white wine estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care international GI tables. 15. Sentinel value -1 is not used in this record; all fields are either measured/estimated numeric values or confirmed zeros.
